texto: The way I see it people don't do what they want to do often enough. They just do some alternative which they'd kind of like to do, which isn't the same thing at all, and as a result that thing isn't enough and they end up depressed and annoyed with everyone else around them.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Will Davis: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “The way I see it people don't do what they want to do often enough. They just do some alternative…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, actions, choices pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e choices.

frase_original: The way I see it people don't do what they want to do often enough. They just do some alternative which they'd kind of like to do, which isn't the same thing at all, and as a result that thing isn't enough and they end up depressed and annoyed with everyone else around them.
